phase1.output:
=== Compile-time Polymorphism ===
Add 2 numbers: 8
Add 3 numbers: 10
Add 2 doubles: 8.8
=== Runtime Polymorphism ===
Woof!
Meow!
=== Upcasting ===
Woof!
=== Downcasting ===
Tail wagging!
This is NOT a Cat
phase1.explanation:
Polymorphism means 'many forms' — one name, many behaviors:

Compile-time Polymorphism (Method Overloading):
• Multiple methods with same name, different parameters
• Resolved at compile time
• Example: Calculator.add() with different parameters

Runtime Polymorphism (Method Overriding):
• Subclass provides specific implementation of parent method
• Resolved at runtime
• Example: Animal reference, Dog/Cat objects

Upcasting: converting subclass to superclass
• Automatic (implicit)
• Dog → Animal
• You lose access to subclass-specific methods

Downcasting: converting superclass to subclass
• Must be explicit: (Dog) animal
• Check with instanceof to avoid ClassCastException

instanceof: checks if object is of certain type
• returns boolean
• Safe way to check before downcasting

phase2.errorMessage:
Exception in thread "main" java.lang.ClassCastException: Dog cannot be cast to Cat
        at Test.main(Test.java:16)
phase2.explanation:
ClassCastException! You tried to cast a Dog to a Cat, but they're different types.

animal is actually a Dog object, but you're trying to treat it as a Cat.

Always use instanceof before downcasting:
if (animal instanceof Cat) {
    Cat cat = (Cat) animal;  // Safe only if instanceof returns true
}

This is why instanceof is important — it prevents runtime crashes!

phase3.prompt:
Create a Payment class with processPayment() method.
Create CreditCard and PayPal subclasses that override processPayment():
• CreditCard: prints "Processing credit card payment of $[amount]"
• PayPal: prints "Processing PayPal payment of $[amount]"

In main, create an array of Payment references with both types.
Loop through and call processPayment(100) on each.
dialogueHints: ProcessPayment should take a double amount parameter
Both subclasses should override the method
Use a Payment[] array to store both types
phase3.openingDialogue: One interface, many forms — that's polymorphism! 🎭
